page: Amazon Web Services i3.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. This dedicated CPU configuration provides 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.25 MB L2 cache, and 45 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 61.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.62 GB per core, and includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the i3.2xlarge exhibits modest compute capabilities. Single-core and multi-core CPU tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block read operations but drop to weak for write operations and small-block reads. Cryptographic and compression tasks, including gzip and bzip3, also fall into the lower performance tiers. However, the instance maintains average performance in Passmark CPU physics and memory scores, as well as text generation for small language models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i3.2xlarge trades raw CPU processing power for high-density local NVMe storage and a substantial memory footprint. This makes it suitable for storage-centric workloads that require fast local read access but do not demand intensive CPU calculations. Appropriate workloads include local database hosting, caching layers, and distributed file systems where the 1900 GB NVMe SSD can be fully leveraged, while intensive computational tasks or high-throughput web serving may be constrained by the legacy processor architecture.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. It features 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 61.0 GB of memory, and a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD for high-capacity local storage. Network performance is established at a 2 Gbps baseline. Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff: while the instance offers substantial local storage and average large-block memory read bandwidth, its computational performance is limited, with single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks scoring in the bottom 10% to 25%. Consequently, the i3.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy applications rather than compute-intensive tasks. It is best suited for workloads such as database operations, local caching, and storage-intensive applications that benefit from direct NVMe access without requiring high-frequency CPU processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB of RAM, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, offering a high-capacity local storage profile with modest CPU performance.

tagline: A storage-focused instance pairing a 1900 GB NVMe SSD with 61 GB of memory for data-intensive local workloads.

61.0 GB of system memory providing 7.62 GB per core
Bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD local storage
